name: Clara Answers — Demo → Agent v1 → Onboarding → Agent v2
description: End-to-end automation pipeline that converts demo call transcripts into preliminary Retell agent configurations (v1), then updates them with onboarding data (v2). Equivalent to an n8n workflow but implemented as a deterministic Python CLI pipeline for zero-cost reproducibility.

trigger.description: The pipeline is triggered by running the CLI command. It processes all accounts defined in data/manifest.json sequentially.

nodes.name: Ingest & Load Manifest
nodes.description: Reads data/manifest.json to discover all accounts and their version entries (v1=demo, v2=onboarding). Validates manifest schema before proceeding.

nodes.name: Read Transcript File
nodes.description: Reads the raw transcript text file specified in each version entry. Normalizes whitespace and extracts the account_id from the filename if needed.

nodes.name: Extract Structured Data
nodes.description: Parses the transcript to extract structured fields: company_name, business_hours, office_address, services_supported, emergency_definition, routing rules, call_transfer_rules, integration_constraints, flow summaries. Supports two modes: (a) rules — deterministic regex extraction (zero-cost, offline), (b) ollama — local LLM extraction via Ollama API (zero-cost, offline).

nodes.name: Normalize & Validate Memo
nodes.description: Post-processes the raw extraction output: normalizes types, strips unsupported/hallucinated fields by checking against transcript text, coerces integers, fills missing required keys with safe defaults (null/[]/{}). Validates against REQUIRED_MEMO_SCHEMA.

nodes.name: Deep Merge with Previous Version
nodes.description: For v2+, merges the new extraction with the previous version's memo using deep_merge_strict(). New non-null/non-empty values override old values; old values are preserved if the new version has null/empty. Prevents accidental data loss.

nodes.name: Build Evidence Map
nodes.description: For each memo field, extracts supporting text snippets from the transcript and assigns a confidence score (0.9 = found in transcript, 0.6 = partially inferred, 0.3 = carried from previous version, 0.0 = not found). Produces evidence.json for auditability.

nodes.name: Generate Agent System Prompt
nodes.description: Generates a complete Retell-compatible system prompt including: Business Hours Flow (greeting → purpose → name/number → route → fallback → anything else → close), After-Hours Flow (greeting → emergency check → collect info → transfer → fallback → close), Call Transfer Protocol, Transfer-Fail Protocol, and General Guidelines.

nodes.name: Build Agent Spec JSON
nodes.description: Assembles the complete Retell Agent Draft Spec including: agent_name, voice_style, system_prompt, key_variables, tool_invocation_placeholders, call_transfer_protocol, transfer_fail_protocol, version, source_type.

nodes.name: Idempotency Check
nodes.description: Computes SHA-256 hash of the memo (excluding updated_at_utc) and compares with the existing output. If unchanged, skips writing to avoid redundant updates. Ensures the pipeline is safe to re-run.

nodes.name: Write Versioned Outputs
nodes.description: Writes all artifacts to outputs/accounts/<account_id>/<version>/: memo.json, agent_spec.json, evidence.json, system_prompt.txt. Creates directories as needed.

nodes.name: Generate Changelog
nodes.description: For v2, compares v1 and v2 memos field-by-field. Produces a human-readable changes.md showing what changed, with nested diff support for business_hours, call_transfer_rules, etc. Only truly changed fields are listed.

nodes.name: Write Summary Report
nodes.description: Writes outputs/summary/report.json with run metadata: timestamps, mode, per-account success/failure, and any errors encountered.

error_handling.description: Each account is processed independently. If one account fails, others continue. Errors are logged and collected in the summary report.

idempotency.description: SHA-256 hash of memo content (excluding timestamps) is compared before writing. Identical content skips disk writes.

batch_capability.description: All accounts in manifest.json are processed in a single batch run. Can be filtered to a single account with --account flag.

n8n_equivalence_note: This workflow is implemented as a Python CLI pipeline rather than n8n nodes because: (1) n8n requires Docker and a running server, adding complexity; (2) the Python implementation is fully deterministic, zero-cost, and reproducible with a single command; (3) the workflow JSON above documents the exact same node-by-node data flow that an n8n workflow would implement. To convert to n8n: each node above maps to an n8n 'Function' or 'Execute Command' node, with the manifest.json trigger replacing an n8n webhook trigger.
